figures.title: A finite measurement kernel determines what spatial disorder is recoverable
figures.purpose: Introduce the observation model, scale-dependent variance, identifiability, and absolute scale-calibration floor as one information hierarchy.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Schematic only: latent x(r), normalized lateral/depth kernel w(r,z), and measured X_w; no specimen units or fitted values.
panels.caption_level_claim: A finite HgCdTe map pixel is a kernel-weighted observation rather than a point sample; finite-aperture mapping itself is established prior art.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The schematic is a measured instrument PSF, a new mapping method, or a universal HgCdTe observation kernel.
panels.rendering_action: Create a clean vector schematic from existing equations and declared composite-kernel components.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Normalized filtered variance V(s)/A versus normalized scale s/xi, with one-scale and two-scale observation markers; dimensionless axes.
panels.caption_level_claim: One scale cannot recover both point variance and correlation length; two Gaussian-benchmark scales estimate them but do not test the covariance family.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The curve is a fit to an HgCdTe specimen or proves Gaussian covariance.
panels.rendering_action: Regenerate from the existing Gaussian benchmark API using the immutable normalized design.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Posterior or uncertainty decomposition for log xi_relative, common log-scale calibration, and log xi_absolute; dimensionless log-length coordinates.
panels.caption_level_claim: A common multiplicative scale error is exactly confounded with absolute correlation length and creates an uncertainty floor that repeats cannot remove.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The displayed uncertainty is a measured HgCdTe correlation-length posterior.
panels.rendering_action: Use the existing local theorem and nonlinear posterior benchmark; show the bounded-prior exception in the caption or inset note.

figures.title: A third scale tests covariance-family closure and exposes misspecification
figures.purpose: Separate parameter estimation from family testing and show how wrong-family fits become scale and convention dependent.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: 1/V versus s^2 for an exact Gaussian benchmark; normalized reciprocal-variance and squared-scale axes.
panels.caption_level_claim: Gaussian covariance requires reciprocal filtered variance to be linear in squared Gaussian-probe scale.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: Reciprocal linearity is a universal property of spatial disorder or its visual appearance proves the family under uncertain kernels.
panels.rendering_action: Render exact line and three declared observation locations from the existing closure API.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Standardized held-out closure residual or z-like distance for Gaussian truth and supported Matérn alternatives; dimensionless residual.
panels.caption_level_claim: Two scales estimate a Gaussian benchmark, while the third supplies the first exact family-closure residual under calibrated uncertainty.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The residuals identify a covariance family in a real wafer or are universal detection thresholds.
panels.rendering_action: Use the immutable three-scale 3-percent-noise benchmark already recorded in R04 state.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Pairwise fitted A/A_true and xi/ell versus selected scale pair for Matérn nu=1/2 truth forced through a Gaussian inverse; dimensionless ratios.
panels.caption_level_claim: A wrong Gaussian family produces scale-pair-dependent variance and correlation-scale estimates.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The synthetic spread is a confidence interval or a measured HgCdTe parameter range.
panels.rendering_action: Regenerate the declared five-scale misspecification benchmark.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Fitted Gaussian parameter locations or family-separation distance under weighted log-variance and reciprocal-variance losses; dimensionless parameter ratios or Mahalanobis distance.
panels.caption_level_claim: When the family is misspecified, fitted parameters depend on the declared loss even when the broad resolved or unresolved classification is unchanged.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: One fitting convention is universally correct or the Mahalanobis distance is a posterior probability.
panels.rendering_action: Select the smallest existing comparison that supports the claim; place the full joint envelope in supplementary material.

figures.title: Finite maps and shared rasters contain far less independent information than their pixel count suggests
figures.purpose: Connect exact finite-map moments to effective information and same-raster cross-scale covariance.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Nominal pixel count and map-mean effective sample count versus pixel spacing at approximately fixed field of view; counts and normalized spacing.
panels.caption_level_claim: Dense resampling can multiply nominal pixel count while adding negligible independent information at fixed field of view.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The effective count is universal for all maps or equals the number of independent experimental repeats.
panels.rendering_action: Use the existing 10 by 10 and 20 by 20 fixed-FOV benchmark.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Expected ordinary sample variance divided by marginal filtered variance versus normalized field size or spacing; dimensionless ratio.
panels.caption_level_claim: Mean subtraction on a correlated finite map can make ordinary sample variance underestimate marginal filtered variance.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The correction creates new information or applies without a declared covariance model.
panels.rendering_action: Use the immutable controlled result and one compact field-size sensitivity curve if already supported by the module.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Heat map of correlation among log map-variance estimators at the declared three scales; dimensionless correlation.
panels.caption_level_claim: Variance estimates from several resolutions of one field can be strongly correlated and cannot be treated as independent scale observations.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The matrix is empirical repeat covariance or applies to arbitrary non-Gaussian maps without qualification.
panels.rendering_action: Render the immutable three-scale correlation matrix.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Parameter uncertainty ellipse or covariance-volume comparison for full same-raster covariance and false independent-pixel/scale treatment; log A and log xi axes.
panels.caption_level_claim: Ignoring within-map and cross-scale dependence can make the inferred parameter covariance volume hundreds of times too small in the controlled design.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The inflation factor is universal or is a measured uncertainty correction for a specific wafer.
panels.rendering_action: Use the controlled 404.6855 covariance-determinant ratio; report parameter-specific SD ratios in the caption.

figures.title: A public CdSeTe PL map demonstrates scale suppression, shared-raster dependence, and finite-field sensitivity
figures.purpose: Exercise the analytical workflow on one real semiconductor observation field while preserving the cross-material and unknown-kernel limitations.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Gaussian-fitted PL peak wavelength over the 24 by 24 registered field; x and y in micrometres, wavelength in nanometres.
panels.caption_level_claim: The admitted field is a source-data-derived CdSeTe PL observation with physical coordinates and an unmeasured native sample-plane kernel.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The colors are composition, intrinsic bandgap truth, or an HgCdTe map.
panels.rendering_action: Plot the immutable admitted field with source attribution and no interpolation beyond display rasterization.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Ordinary sample variance in nm^2 versus added Gaussian sigma in pixels; optional secondary coordinate conversion in micrometres explicitly labeled as added-kernel conversion only.
panels.caption_level_claim: Added smoothing reduces apparent map variance to 11.24 percent of the unsmoothed value at four pixels.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: The x axis is the native effective PSF or the curve yields a physical material correlation length.
panels.rendering_action: Plot the five immutable variance values and retained fractions.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Training log-RMS and held-out one-pixel relative variance error for Gaussian and supported Matérn families; dimensionless residuals.
panels.caption_level_claim: Matérn nu=3/2 has the smallest residual under the frozen convention, but the comparison is descriptive and not independent family validation.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: Matérn nu=3/2 is the CdSeTe or HgCdTe material covariance law.
panels.rendering_action: Render the immutable four-family residual table as a compact point or bar plot.

panels.plotted_variables_and_units: Maximum relative variance-curve change for wrap boundary, nearest boundary, central crop, and planar detrending relative to the primary reflect convention; percent.
panels.caption_level_claim: Boundary and crop choices change the variance curve by about 72.7 percent, exceeding the descriptive family separation and preventing a family claim from this map.
panels.prohibited_interpretation: One edge convention is physically correct or the sensitivity invalidates the analytical theory.
panels.rendering_action: Plot the four immutable maximum relative differences and state the primary convention.
